privileged_info_summary: The task uses Juris-M (Jurism), a real open-source legal reference manager, to export a collection containing exactly 2 real Supreme Court cases: New York Times Co. v. Sullivan (376 U.S. 254, 1964) and Tinker v. Des Moines (393 U.S. 503, 1969). The test library contains 10 total items: 7 real Supreme Court cases (Brown v. Board, Marbury v. Madison, Miranda v. Arizona, Sullivan, Gideon v. Wainwright, Obergefell v. Hodges, Tinker) and 3 law review articles. Two of the articles are real published works: Holmes' 'The Path of the Law' (Harvard Law Review, 1897) and Monaghan's 'Constitutional Fact Review' (Columbia Law Review, 1985). However, the third article attributed to Ronald D. Poe in Yale Law Journal (1971) could not be verified and appears to be synthetic data. The expected output file is /home/ga/Documents/first_amendment_refs.bib containing only the 2 collection cases.
